title: Altenglische Legenden: Neue Folge
summary: The repository XML derives from U-M’s 2006 uncorrected OCR/TEI encoding of Carl Horstmann’s 1881 multi-witness edited selection. This graph distinguishes the repository artifact, encoding, complete printed edition, U-M and Internet Archive surrogates, direct manuscript/early-print sources, comparison witnesses, the Hickes intermediary, a reported lost Trinity witness, and the DIMEV v1.0 crosswalk. Ten cached objects contain reproducible target portions; seven supporting/catalog or zero-overlap objects explicitly mark the target absent.
review_notes: The source chain is edition-centered: afw1383 is not one codex or one medieval work.
Horstmann’s historical Barbour label is preserved but not accepted as whole-selection or Saint Machor authorship.
The U-M page-image layer contains more positions than unique source images; 708 positions and 674 unique hashes are both retained as true statements.
Public access, target presence, source completeness, and reuse permission are modeled separately.

Full DIMEV v1.0 crosswalk completed for all sixty-five counted verse items: 64 matched, 1 unmatched (Saint Peter in Chains), 67 unique matched records, and 70 part-to-record mappings. Exact selected-source witness rows and all boundary overlaps are preserved in the paired metadata manifest.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for afw1383
entities.description: The repository artifact consumed by the publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan electronic text AFW1383.0001.001
entities.description: U-M’s 2006 uncorrected OCR and TEI-in-Libraries Level 1 encoding of Horstmann’s selected text and apparatus, with linked page images.
notes: The source header declares only German; the encoded content also contains Middle English, Older Scots, and embedded Latin.
The inherited Barbour bibliographic attribution is not accepted as whole-selection authorship.

entities.label: Carl Horstmann, Altenglische Legenden: Neue Folge (1881)
entities.description: The complete printed scholarly edition and immediate source of the U-M encoding. It selects medieval texts from many witnesses, retains German introductions and apparatus, and includes a poem in the notes.

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, Altenglische Legenden: Neue Folge, mit Einleitung und Anmerkungen (Heilbronn: Gebr. Henninger, 1881).
bibliographic.title: Altenglische Legenden: Neue Folge

entities.label: University of Michigan page-image sequence for AFW1383.0001.001
entities.description: A 708-position U-M Presentation 2 sequence of the printed edition. Checksum audit finds 674 unique image payloads, exactly reconciling the XML header’s 674 TIFF source images.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 with JPEG Image API derivatives

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Horstmann 1881
entities.description: Complete 686-image scan of the printed edition, including covers and front/back matter. Its internal scan sequence has a blank early image and omitted printed pages 3 and 169–170, so target PDF locators are recorded explicitly.

entities.label: Internet Archive machine OCR for Horstmann 1881
entities.description: Machine-generated plain text and page-structured DjVu XML for the complete Internet Archive scan; suitable for discovery and boundary checks, not authoritative transcription.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 4196
entities.description: Principal witness for the Northern Legendary sequence; Horstmann begins the represented collection at manuscript fol. 133 and reports that the later Harley hand copies Cotton Tiberius E VII.

date_statements.label: repository date
date_statements.value: fourth quarter of the fourteenth century–first quarter of the fifteenth century

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Tiberius E VII, Part II
entities.description: Comparison witness for the Northern Legendary; damaged in the 1731 Cotton Library fire.

date_statements.label: repository date
date_statements.value: first half of the fifteenth century

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Ashmole 42
entities.description: Source of the appended Peter and Paul sermon and one Saint Alexius version, at Horstmann’s reported f. 281v and associated loci.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.5.31
entities.description: Source of Horstmann’s second Saint Alexius version; the edition’s abbreviated Gg. V, 31 is retained as a historical designation.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.2.6
entities.description: Source of the Older Scots Saint Machor at Horstmann’s f. 190v. The historical label ‘Barbour’s Legendensammlung’ is not accepted as secure John Barbour authorship.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Digby 86
entities.description: Direct source for Saint Eustace (ff. 122v–125v), Sayings of Bede (ff. 127v–130r), and Our Lady’s Psalter (ff. 130r–132r).

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Ashmole 61
entities.description: Direct or comparison source for eight discontiguous target ranges from ff. 26v–150v, including Margaret, exempla, the Crucifix, Creation, Stations, two hell stories, Eustace variants, and Ypotys variants.

entities.label: National Library of Scotland, Advocates MS 19.2.1 (Auchinleck Manuscript)
entities.description: Source for Saint Margaret, one Saint Katherine version, the Marian clerk fragment, and lacunae supplied in Our Lady’s Psalter.

entities.label: Gonville and Caius College MS 175/96
entities.description: Source of Horstmann’s second version of Saint Katherine, cited at p. 107.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38
entities.description: Source of the separately printed Saint Katherine version, item no. 24.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2250
entities.description: Sole witness of Saint Erkenwald at current ff. 72v–75v; Horstmann’s old foliation gives f. 72b.

date_statements.label: repository date
date_statements.value: second half of the fifteenth century

entities.label: British Library, Royal MS 17 C XVII
entities.description: Source of The Holy Blood of Hailes, cited by Horstmann at p. 147.

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Faustina B III component ff. 199r–264v
entities.description: Source of Saint Etheldreda from f. 260; the ending is lost in the manuscript component.

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Nero C XII component ff. 183r–188v
entities.description: Current catalog component containing Saints Wulfhad and Ruffin at ff. 183r–188v. Horstmann’s legacy label ‘Nero CXII fol. 181’ is preserved but not normalized into a false shelfmark.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2382
entities.description: Source of The Child of Bristowe at f. 118v.

entities.label: William Copland print of The Smith and His Dame (unique Bodleian copy)
entities.description: Horstmann’s base printed witness, a unique undated early-sixteenth-century Copland edition held as Bodleian 4to Seld. C.39; its missing leaf removes verses 114–180.
notes: Modeled as an early printed witness, not as a manuscript.

entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde fragment of The Smith and His Dame
entities.description: Two half-leaf fragments of another edition, printed in London about 1505 and found at Gonville and Caius College; Horstmann uses them to supply selected lines from the Copland copy’s lacuna.
notes: Modeled as an early printed witness, not as a manuscript.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.5.48
entities.description: Base witness of A Tale of an Incestuous Daughter at f. 44.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon Manuscript)
entities.description: Base witness of Ipotis at current ff. 296va–297vc.

entities.label: British Library, Add MS 22283 (Simeon Manuscript)
entities.description: Younger comparison witness used for Ipotis variants.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2255
entities.description: Source of John Lydgate’s Saint Giles at Horstmann’s f. 95b.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2278
entities.description: Presentation manuscript and principal source of Lydgate’s Saint Edmund and Saint Fremund; dated 1433–1434.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Ashmole 46
entities.description: Alternate later recension of Saint Edmund and Saint Fremund beginning at f. 87 and adding a 463-line miracle. The currently public two-image selection stops at ff. 1r and 4v and does not contain the target.

entities.label: Durham University Library, Cosin MS V.ii.14
entities.description: Source of Lydgate’s Saint Margaret at Horstmann’s f. 97b.

entities.label: Lincoln Cathedral MS 91 (Thornton Manuscript)
entities.description: Source of Saint Christopher, Saint John the Evangelist, the Thornton Marian fragment, and Lamentacio peccatoris at Horstmann’s ff. 122b, 231, 147, and 51b respectively.

entities.label: Reported Trinity College Cambridge witness of Meidan Maregrete
entities.description: The manuscript behind Hickes’s 1705 print of Meidan Maregrete. Horstmann names only a Trinity College Cambridge manuscript; no defensible current shelfmark was identified.
notes: Reported lost/unidentified is retained as uncertainty; Horstmann is not linked directly to a guessed modern witness.

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Caligula A II
entities.description: Base witness of Ypotys at Horstmann’s f. 79b.

entities.label: British Library, Arundel MS 140
entities.description: Comparison witness for Ypotys, cited from f. 1.

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Titus A XXVI
entities.description: Comparison witness for Ypotys, cited at f. 163; its opening is absent.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Ashmole 750
entities.description: A younger Ypotys witness described by Horstmann but not used for the printed collation.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Douce 323
entities.description: A younger Ypotys witness described by Horstmann but not used for the printed collation.

entities.label: George Hickes, Linguarum Veterum Septentrionalium Thesaurus, volume 1 (1705)
entities.description: Intermediary printed source for Meidan Maregrete at pp. 224–231. Hickes, rather than Horstmann, reports and transcribes the now-unidentified Trinity College witness.

bibliographic.citation: George Hickes, Linguarum Vett. Septentrionalium Thesaurus Grammatico-Criticus et Archaeologicus, vol. 1 (Oxford, 1705).

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Hickes 1705, volume 1
entities.description: Complete 796-image scan; Meidan Maregrete appears at printed pp. 224–231 / PDF images 328–335.

Digital Bodleian complete facsimile of MS Ashmole 61
Digital Bodleian complete facsimile of the Vernon Manuscript
Digital Bodleian two-image selection from MS Ashmole 46
entities.description: The complete currently exposed two-image digital object contains ff. 1r and 4v only; the afw1383 target begins at f. 87 and is absent.

entities.description: Complete inventory of all 701 images publicly exposed through the official Auchinleck project, including detached fragments.
entities.reproduction_format: Exhaustive provider Image API URL inventory

entities.label: Lincoln Cathedral public image of Thornton Manuscript f. 80r
entities.description: The one catalog image publicly retrievable from the current record. Visual inspection confirms that f. 80r is outside all afw1383 target folios.

entities.label: DIMEV v1.0 crosswalk for Horstmann’s sixty-five-item selection
entities.description: A complete item-by-item repertory crosswalk of the sixty-five counted verse items in afw1383. Sixty-four items match sixty-seven unique DIMEV records, yielding seventy part-to-record mappings because several Horstmann items and DIMEV records have non-isomorphic boundaries. Saint Peter in Chains is the sole unmatched item.

identifiers.value: record-78, record-317, record-364, record-374, record-383, record-398, record-474, record-706, record-720, record-846, record-864, record-1387, record-1535, record-1600, record-1613, record-1645, record-1788, record-1880, record-1881, record-1882, record-2002, record-2030, record-2042, record-2572, record-2591, record-2652, record-2803, record-2809, record-2864, record-3030, record-3036, record-3243, record-3246, record-3348, record-3468, record-3911, record-4129, record-4132, record-4185, record-4189, record-4190, record-4248, record-4249, record-4529, record-4538, record-4539, record-4546, record-4568, record-4619, record-4624, record-4664, record-4698, record-4718, record-4736, record-4747, record-4748, record-4751, record-4757, record-4759, record-4766, record-4815, record-4914, record-4974, record-5422, record-5603, record-5690, record-5800

notes: The sixty-five Horstmann item count is retained. DIMEV record boundaries are collateral identifiers, not replacement item boundaries.
The eight-line uncounted Northern prologue matches record-2003 / wit-2003-1 and remains item_count 0.
Records 755 and 960 occur only as Middle English quotations in Horstmann’s introductory front matter; they are not silently added to the sixty-five counted text items.

Boundary overlaps are explicit: record-2652 spans the separately modeled Sixtus and Lawrence items; record-383 spans the Vernon Ipotis and Caligula Ypotys versions; record-317 spans the Ashmole William Basterdfeld text and Thornton Lamentacio; the Ashmole Peter-and-Paul sermon contains record-2591 inside record-5800; the combined Katherine item maps to records 1882 and 1881; and Horstmann’s Edmund/Fremund item maps to five records.

DIMEV’s Horstmann1881a references contain several anomalies checked against the edition and source incipits: record-364 gives pp. 22–35 for the pp. 225–235 Auchinleck Margaret; record-5690 gives p. 186 for the pp. 147–152 All Souls item; record-5800 attaches pp. 77–92 to the Huntington row although Horstmann’s selected sermon is Ashmole 42 at pp. 77–81; and record-3348 omits Horstmann despite an exact Ashmole 61 incipit, locus, and text match.

That member was re-retrieved from its inventory image URL, matched the inventory’s 485,272-byte size and SHA-256 87c79c5c79e6e25556e8d82fb4c8c502d56244c37d37ca166eff9e75bdb6f507, and the rebuilt 710-member archive passes both unzip and 7-Zip integrity tests.

rights.component: Repository XML and markup
rights.basis: The pinned LICENSE speaks of software and compiled binaries. Its application to the corpus XML, markup, and transcribed content is unclear, so no public-domain dedication or license is inferred for those layers.

rights.component: U-M digital text and encoded edition content
rights.basis: The current U-M/DPLA record marks the item Public Domain / No Copyright–United States. This does not extend to independently supplied manuscript photographs.

rights.component: 1881 printed edition text and typography
rights.basis: Published in 1881 and therefore in the U.S. public domain; this does not itself license modern scans or manuscript photography.

rights.component: U-M page-image delivery of the 1881 volume
rights.basis: The U-M/DPLA item-level statement is No Copyright–United States; provider access terms still apply.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files and delivery layer
rights.basis: The underlying 1881 edition is public domain in the United States, but the checked IA metadata supplies no rights, license, or usage field for the scan layer.

rights.component: Internet Archive machine OCR and DjVu XML
rights.basis: The checked IA metadata gives no explicit license for the generated delivery files; underlying edition content is separately assessed.

rights.component: DIMEV v1.0 data, documentation, and schemas
rights.basis: The release README and bundled LICENSE apply Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Multiple manuscript and early-print witnesses, plus a poem embedded in notes
editorial_practices.representation: Sixty-five cataloged medieval verse text items arranged in two principal sequences, appendices, and back notes
editorial_practices.description: Horstmann selects, groups, and sometimes juxtaposes witness versions; the manifest preserves the selection boundary instead of treating the volume as one medieval work.

editorial_practices.representation: Footnote variants, supplied lacunae, paired versions, and separately printed recensions
editorial_practices.description: Direct source transcription is distinguished from witnesses merely collated or cited; described younger Ypotys manuscripts are not promoted to direct sources.
editorial_practices.scope: Northern Legendary variants and item-specific comparison apparatus

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/afw1383.xml.
evidence.locator: HEADER; TITLEPAGE; complete FRONT, BODY, and BACK
evidence.summary: Identifies AFW1383.0001.001 and preserves the selection hierarchy, source headings, page/folio milestones, medieval texts, variants, and notes poem.

evidence.citation: Reproducible XML structural and checksum audit of afw1383.xml.
evidence.locator: Element counts, division tree, and SHA-256
evidence.summary: Counts 11 DIV1, 66 DIV2, 37 DIV3, 1,013 LG, 34,045 L, 168 MILESTONE, 108 P, and 708 PB occurrences. The PB elements contain 674 unique REF values: 640 occur once and 34 occur twice, reconciling the header’s 674 source TIFFs without inflating the carrier. The audit also distinguishes 65 verse items, the framing prologue, zero-line editorial headings, split stories, paired versions, and the back-notes poem.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Altenglische legenden, AFW1383.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Supports item identity, title, call number, editor, 1881 publication, 2006 OCR/TEI history, linked page images, and public access.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan IIIF Presentation manifest for cme:afw1383.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Exposes the complete 708-position page-image sequence and exact canvas/image services.

evidence.citation: Checksum and inventory audit of the complete U-M AFW1383 IIIF bundle.
evidence.locator: manifest.json; inventory.json; 708 image members; complete archive CRC test
evidence.summary: Finds 674 unique image payloads among 708 positions, with 34 duplicate pairs, exactly reconciling the XML header’s 674 source TIFF images; confirms target canvas ranges and retained non-target page-image matter. After one corrupt member was replaced from its exact inventory URL and verified against its recorded byte count and SHA-256, all 710 archive members pass unzip and 7-Zip integrity tests.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan/DPLA rights statement for Altenglische legenden.
evidence.summary: The aggregated item record reports Public Domain / No Copyright–United States while preserving the distinction between access and provider terms.

evidence.citation: Carl Horstmann, Altenglische Legenden: Neue Folge (Heilbronn, 1881), complete volume.
evidence.locator: title page; pp. 1–471, 488–530; source headnotes and apparatus
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the edition identity, selection boundary, printed pages, direct sources, comparison witnesses, intermediary Hickes chain, German apparatus, item divisions, and historical designations.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for altenglischeleg01barbgoog.
evidence.summary: Confirms item identifier, title, creator, 1881 date, exact derivative filenames and sizes; rights, licenseurl, and usage fields are absent.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: Complete 1,211-byte root LICENSE; git blob fdddb29aa445bf3d6a5d843d6dd77e10a9f99657
evidence.summary: Direct inspection of the pinned tree confirms the root LICENSE and its software-oriented wording, including software and compiled binaries. It establishes the repository-level assertion but does not clearly establish that inherited corpus XML, markup, or transcribed source content is dedicated under the Unlicense.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS. Ashmole 42, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries.
evidence.summary: Supports the current shelfmark, 1340–1360 date, and manuscript identity; no complete public Digital Bodleian object was found.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS. Digby 86, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries and Digital Bodleian.
evidence.summary: Supports the current shelfmark, codex description, complete 430-canvas object, and mapped target folios.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS. Ashmole 61, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries and Digital Bodleian.
evidence.summary: Supports current shelfmark, codex description, complete 358-canvas object, and discontiguous target folios.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Vernon Manuscript, MS. Eng. poet. a. 1.
evidence.summary: Supports current shelfmark, late-fourteenth-century codex, complete 710-canvas object, and Ipotis at ff. 296v–297v images.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS. Ashmole 46, catalog and Digital Bodleian object.
evidence.summary: Supports the manuscript identity and currently exposed two-image object; those images are ff. 1r and 4v, while the target begins at f. 87.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF attribution and rights statements for the cached manuscript objects.
evidence.summary: Provider manifests state Photo © Bodleian Libraries and CC BY-NC 4.0; the cached image layer retains attribution and noncommercial conditions.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland, Advocates MS 19.2.1 (Auchinleck Manuscript), Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue.
evidence.summary: Supports current shelfmark, 1331–1340 date, manuscript identity, and project relationship.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland, Auchinleck Manuscript project and complete contents/facsimile links.
evidence.summary: Supports the exhaustive 701-image published inventory and exact target surfaces for Margaret, Katherine, the Marian fragment, and Psalter supplement.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland, Auchinleck Manuscript project copyright and permission terms.
evidence.summary: Public research access does not waive project publication and commercial-use permission requirements.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Special Collections catalog and access guidance.
evidence.summary: Supports current institution/shelfmark practice and the request route for Gg.5.31, Ff.2.38, and Ff.5.48; exact target foliation remains grounded in Horstmann.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.2.6, historical university catalog description.
evidence.summary: Supports the Gg.2.6 identity, fifteenth-century Scottish Legendary context, and current shelfmark continuity.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Gonville and Caius College, description of MS 175/96.
evidence.summary: Supports the historical/current Caius 175/96 identity; Horstmann supplies the Saint Katherine source locus at p. 107.

evidence.citation: Durham University Library, Cosin MS V.ii.14.
evidence.summary: Supports the current shelfmark and codex description; Horstmann supplies the Saint Margaret locus.

evidence.citation: Lincoln Cathedral Library, MS 91 (Thornton Manuscript), CalmView catalog record.
evidence.summary: Supports the current manuscript identity and public catalog image; target loci are retained from Horstmann pending repository foliation confirmation.

evidence.citation: Lincoln Cathedral MS 91 public catalog image, f. 80r.
evidence.summary: The single public JPEG is f. 80r and lies outside target ff. 51b, 122b, 147, and 231; target_work_presence is therefore absent.

evidence.citation: Lincoln Cathedral, Readers and Researchers.
evidence.summary: Provides onsite consultation and reproduction enquiry route; no reusable image license is asserted.

evidence.citation: George Hickes, Linguarum Veterum Septentrionalium Thesaurus, volume 1 (1705), complete scan.
evidence.summary: Supports Meidan Maregrete’s intermediary print, target pagination, and Hickes’s report of a Trinity College Cambridge manuscript.

evidence.citation: British Library Imaging Services order form, service description, and terms.
evidence.summary: Supports the request route, fee/feasibility uncertainty, and separate reuse permission for commissioned images.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 4196.
evidence.summary: Supports shelfmark, 1375–1425 date, physical extent, and current lack of a complete public image object.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Tiberius E VII.
evidence.summary: Supports shelfmark, first-half-fifteenth-century date, fire-damaged state, and current catalog access.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 2250.
evidence.summary: Supports Saint Erkenwald at ff. 72v–75v, second-half-fifteenth-century date, and unavailable legacy images.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Royal MS 17 C XVII.
evidence.summary: Supports current shelfmark and codex identity.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Faustina B III component.
evidence.summary: Supports current component identity, date context, and target-bearing ff. 199r–264v component.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Nero C XII component ff. 183r–188v.
evidence.summary: Corrects the malformed legacy label and supports target component ff. 183r–188v.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 2382.
evidence.summary: Supports current shelfmark and codex identity.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 22283.
evidence.summary: Supports current shelfmark and Simeon Manuscript identity.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 2255.
evidence.summary: Supports current shelfmark and catalog identity.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 2278.
evidence.summary: Supports current shelfmark, 1433–1434 date, presentation-manuscript context, and currently unavailable old full images.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Caligula A II.
evidence.summary: Supports current shelfmark and codex identity.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Arundel MS 140.
evidence.summary: Supports current shelfmark and codex identity.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Titus A XXVI.
evidence.summary: Supports current shelfmark and codex identity.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, “What Is Copyright?” and Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.locator: Public-domain and duration statements current in 2026
evidence.summary: Works published before January 1, 1931 are in the U.S. public domain. This evidence is used only for the 1881 and 1705 editions, early printed witnesses, and underlying medieval wording, not for later encodings, scans, photographs, catalog descriptions, or contractual access layers.

evidence.citation: UK Intellectual Property Office, Copyright Notice: Duration of copyright (term), published 15 January 2021.
evidence.locator: Exceptions to the general rules: Pre-1989 unpublished works (the 2039 rule); Common questions: Are old literary works still within copyright?
evidence.summary: Official guidance says some literary works unpublished at the end of 1988 remain protected in the United Kingdom through 31 December 2039 even when their authors died centuries ago. The exact witness wording’s valid publication or public-availability history must therefore be established before assigning a UK public-domain status.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, complete afw1383 item and witness-row crosswalk.
evidence.locator: data/Records.xml; data/Manuscripts.xml; data/MSSIndex.xml; data/PrintedBooks.xml; data/Bibliography.xml; README.md; LICENSE
evidence.summary: Complete row-level crosswalk: 64 of 65 counted items matched to 67 unique records through 70 part-to-record mappings; Saint Peter in Chains had no exact v1.0 match. Record-2003 identifies the uncounted prologue. Records 755 and 960 are front-matter quotations, not additional counted items. The release also exposes the explicitly recorded overlap and edition-citation anomalies documented in the crosswalk entity and part locators.

open_questions.question: What current shelfmark, if any, corresponds to Hickes’s Trinity College Cambridge witness of Meidan Maregrete?
next_steps: Compare Hickes’s phrasing with historical and current Trinity catalogs; ask Trinity Library before assigning any modern identifier.
notes: Until resolved, retain the three-layer chain Horstmann → Hickes → reported Trinity manuscript.

open_questions.question: Can complete public or commissioned whole-object facsimiles be obtained for the uncached British Library, Cambridge, Durham, Lincoln, and early-print sources?
next_steps: Use the recorded repository routes; request covers, bindings, endleaves, blanks, every folio/page, and target-locus confirmation rather than work-only crops.

open_questions.question: What reuse permissions govern the IA scan/OCR, Lincoln catalog image, and any future commissioned manuscript photography?
next_steps: Obtain provider-specific written terms before redistribution; do not infer a license from public access or underlying public-domain text.

open_questions.question: What non-U.S. copyright status applies to the exact medieval witness wording, the Horstmann and Hickes editions, the corpus XML/markup, and later digital layers?
next_steps: Establish whether each exact witness text was validly made available to the public before the end of 1988; a different version’s publication does not by itself settle the witness wording’s UK status under the 2039 rule.
Assess the relevant publication, author/editor death, typographical-arrangement, database, and markup rules jurisdiction by jurisdiction.
Do not project the U.S. public-domain conclusions or a software-oriented repository license into the United Kingdom or worldwide.
Keep provider contracts and modern photography, scan, catalog, and encoding layers separate from underlying medieval wording.
